reason: The remediation only edits PAM password quality configuration to set a minimum password length, a standard STIG hardening step. It does not modify kernel, bootloader, or filesystem layout, and the change can be applied without reboot. While an overly high minlen could lock out users, the fix follows typical patterns and can be tested safely.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential user lockout if minlen is set higher than existing passwords allow; Misconfiguration could cause authentication failures
